---
title: "1.13 Object Creation and Storage"
subtitle: "Using Objects and Methods"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Constructors

### How objects come into existence

Primitive variables store values directly.  
Object variables store <span class="term">references</span>, and a usable object is created by a <span class="term">constructor</span>.

```java
World habitat = new World();
Turtle yertle = new Turtle(habitat);
```

The keyword <span class="mark">`new`</span> creates the object and calls the constructor with the matching signature.

## World Constructors

### Default size or chosen size

![World constructor signatures](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-13-object-creation-and-storage/worldConstructors.png){fig-align="center" width="58%"}

::: {.tight-list}
- `World()` is a <span class="term">no-argument constructor</span>
- `World(int width, int height)` builds a world with chosen dimensions
- constructors are <span class="term">overloaded</span> when the parameter lists differ
:::

```java
World world1 = new World();
World world2 = new World(300, 400);
```

## Turtle Constructors

### Required arguments and argument order

```java
Turtle t1 = new Turtle(world1);
Turtle t2 = new Turtle(50, 100, world1);
```

::: {.tight-list}
- `Turtle` always needs a world so it has somewhere to draw
- some constructors use only the world
- some constructors use <span class="mark">`x, y, world`</span> in that exact order
- wrong argument order gives a type mismatch or incorrect result
:::

![Turtle constructor signatures](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-13-object-creation-and-storage/TurtleClassDefn.png){fig-align="center" width="66%"}

## Object Variables and References

### A variable can refer to an object or to `null`

```java
World world1 = new World();
Turtle t1 = null;
t1 = new Turtle(world1);
```

::: {.tight-list}
- a reference variable does <span class="mark">not</span> contain the whole object
- it stores a way to find that object
- `null` means the variable does not refer to any object yet
- calling a method on `null` causes a runtime error
:::

## Reading Constructor Signatures

### Parameters tell you what the constructor expects

::: {.two-col}
![Date constructors](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-13-object-creation-and-storage/DateClass.png){width="100%"}
![Parameter mapping](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-13-object-creation-and-storage/parameterMappingDate.png){width="100%"}
:::

The <span class="term">signature</span> shows the constructor name and parameter list.

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">parameters</span> appear in the constructor header
- <span class="term">arguments</span> are the values you pass in the call
- types and order must match the signature
:::

## Call by Value

### Arguments are copied into parameters

```java
Date birthday = new Date(2020, 9, 20);
```

When the constructor runs:

::: {.tight-list}
- `2020`, `9`, and `20` are <span class="term">arguments</span>
- `year`, `month`, and `day` are <span class="term">parameters</span>
- Java uses <span class="term">call by value</span>, so the parameter variables receive copies
- after the constructor finishes, control returns to the next statement after the call
:::

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- syntax checks for valid constructor calls
- argument-order debugging with `World` and `Turtle`
- reference-versus-`null` quick checks
- <span class="term">1.13.6 Coding Challenge: Custom Turtles</span>
:::

![Custom turtles challenge](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-13-object-creation-and-storage/customTurtles.png){fig-align="center" width="42%"}

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain that constructors create and initialize objects
- distinguish overloaded constructors by their parameter lists
- explain that object variables store references
- recognize `null` as no object reference
- distinguish <span class="term">parameters</span> from <span class="term">arguments</span>
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
